name: Zhang 2025 PDE5 Inhibitor Vascular Function Meta-Analysis Extract
population_or_context: Randomized controlled trials of PDE5 inhibitors versus placebo across mixed populations and vascular endpoints.
methods: Systematic review and meta-analysis of RCTs published from 1998 to 2022, pooling mean differences for blood pressure, carotid intima-media thickness, pulse wave velocity, flow-mediated dilation, peak systolic velocity, endothelial progenitor cells, endothelial microparticles, augmentation index, brachial artery diameter, and reactive hyperemia index.
population_profile: Mixed RCT populations and PDE5 agents; not specific to healthy men age 30 to 50 or tadalafil-only exposure.
tadalafil_exposure: Mixed PDE5 inhibitors including tadalafil and non-tadalafil agents; tadalafil-specific subgroup details were not extracted in this baseline.
indication_context: Class-level vascular-function evidence used as adjacent evidence for tadalafil hypotheses.
study_design: Systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized placebo-controlled trials.
safety_findings: Abstract focuses on vascular efficacy parameters, not detailed adverse events; safety must be interpreted from tadalafil-specific safety baseline.
directness_to_target_population: Low to moderate: RCT evidence on vascular endpoints, but mixed agents, mixed populations, and surrogate outcomes limit direct transfer.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Class-level pooling, heterogeneity across drugs, doses, indications and endpoints, surrogate outcomes, and no proof of cardiovascular event prevention.
class_transfer_limits: Class evidence supports PDE5 vascular plausibility but cannot erase tadalafil-specific dose, half-life, safety, indication, population, and endpoint limits.
